senses.definition: the process or action of joining a school, course, etc.
a formal request, usually written, for permission to do something, such as getting a job, studying at a university, etc.

examples.x: His university application was accepted.
The deadline for the scholarship application is next week.
You need to fill out an online application to register.
The company received hundreds of applications for the position.

senses.definition: a written statement outlining a person's achievements, interests, and goals, often used as part of a college or job application

examples.x: Her personal statement highlighted her passion for environmental conservation.
Writing a compelling personal statement is essential for a successful college application.
The applicant's personal statement detailed their experiences volunteering abroad.
In his personal statement, he discussed how his upbringing influenced his career aspirations.
The admissions committee was impressed by the depth and sincerity of her personal statement.

senses.definition: a standardized college admission application primarily used in the United States by multiple institutions to streamline the admissions process for undergraduate applicants

examples.x: The Common Application simplifies the college admission process for students applying to multiple universities in the United States.
Many colleges and universities across the U.S. accept the Common Application, making it a widely recognized tool for undergraduate admissions.
Students can use the Common Application to provide information about their academic achievements, extracurricular activities, and personal background.
The Common Application aims to reduce the administrative burden on both applicants and admissions offices, facilitating a more efficient and standardized approach to college admissions.
The Common Application serves as a centralized platform for students to streamline the submission process of their college applications to multiple institutions in the United States.

senses.definition: a formal letter informing an individual of their acceptance into a program, school, or organization

examples.x: She eagerly awaited the acceptance letter from her dream university.
Receiving the acceptance letter was a moment of pure joy for the applicant.
After months of anticipation, the acceptance letter finally arrived in the mail.
He proudly displayed his acceptance letter on the refrigerator for all to see.
The school sent out acceptance letters to all successful candidates.

senses.definition: the permission given to someone to become a student of a school, enter an organization, etc.

examples.x: She received her letter of admission to the university, confirming her acceptance into the engineering program.
Admission to the club requires filling out an application and attending an interview with the board members.
He was thrilled to receive admission to his top-choice graduate school after months of hard work on his application.
The museum offers free admission to children under the age of twelve.
Admission to the concert is included with the purchase of a festival pass.

senses.definition: a college admissions process where students apply to a single institution early in their senior year and commit to attending if accepted

examples.x: The student applied to Harvard University through early decision in hopes of securing a spot in their dream school.
She was relieved when she got accepted through early decision to her top-choice university.
Early decision applications typically have a higher acceptance rate than regular decision applicants.
Despite the pressure, he decided to go with early decision because he was certain about his chosen university.
The university's early decision deadline is November 1st, so make sure to submit your application before then.

senses.definition: the group of individuals admitted to a program or institution during a specific period

examples.x: The university had a large intake of freshmen this year, resulting in crowded dormitories.
The intake for the engineering program was limited due to a shortage of faculty members.
The university offers multiple intakes throughout the year to accommodate different schedules.
The shelter's intake of homeless individuals has increased significantly following recent economic downturns.

senses.definition: the formal enrollment process at a university or college

examples.x: Have you finished your matriculation yet?
Matriculation confirms your acceptance to the institution.
I completed my matriculation last week.
Students must complete matriculation before starting courses.

senses.definition: a roster of individuals who are queued or in line for a particular service, opportunity, or item

examples.x: Due to high demand, there is a long waiting list for tickets to the concert.
She was disappointed to learn that there was a waiting list for the popular elective courses.
The restaurant informed us that there was a waiting list for reservations, but we decided to wait for a table to become available.
The waiting list for affordable housing in the city continues to grow due to limited availability.
Despite applying early, he was placed on the waiting list for admission to his preferred college.

senses.definition: the geographical area from which a hospital, school, or business draws its clientele or customers

examples.x: The new shopping mall's catchment area includes several nearby neighborhoods.
The catchment area of the school extends to several towns in the region.
The hospital's catchment area covers a large portion of the city and surrounding suburbs.
The catchment area of the community center serves residents from multiple districts.
The catchment area of the park attracts visitors from all over the county.

senses.definition: the process of choosing classes or subjects to study within a curriculum

examples.x: Students must complete their course selection for the upcoming semester by next Friday.
Course selection for next year's curriculum will begin next month.
The guidance counselor helped me with my course selection for my senior year.
Parents are encouraged to be involved in their child's course selection process.
The online portal allows students to easily make changes to their course selection throughout the registration period.

senses.definition: an identification card for students, typically containing their name, photo, and identification number

examples.x: The student ID card provides discounts at local businesses.
You'll need your student ID card to check out library books.
The student ID card serves as proof of enrollment for student discounts on public transportation.

senses.definition: an examination of a student's academic progress and requirements to ensure they meet the criteria for graduation

examples.x: Before graduating, students must complete a degree audit to confirm they have fulfilled all requirements.
The advisor conducted a degree audit to help the student plan their remaining courses.
The university offers an online tool for students to track their degree progress through degree audits.
John was relieved to find out that his degree audit showed he only needed one more class to graduate.
Completing a degree audit early can help students avoid last-minute surprises before graduation.

senses.definition: the action of successfully finishing studies at a high school or a university degree

examples.x: Graduation marks an important milestone in every student’s life.
The school held a party to celebrate the students’ graduation.
Graduation is often a time for reflecting on personal achievements.
The university organizes two graduations each year.
She felt proud to walk across the stage at her graduation.

senses.definition: a book containing photographs, signatures, and personal messages from members of a graduating class, typically distributed at the end of the academic year

examples.x: She spent hours designing the cover for the school yearbook, which featured a collage of memorable moments from the academic year.
Students eagerly awaited the release of the yearbook, eager to see their photographs and relive cherished memories.
The yearbook committee worked tirelessly to compile photographs and write-ups for each student and faculty member.
Each graduating senior received a copy of the yearbook as a memento of their time at the school.
The yearbook served as a valuable historical record, documenting the achievements and milestones of the school community throughout the year.
